assumptions: No Bird workspace, AccessKey, or SMS channel exists.
A terminal with cURL is available.

developer_goal: Create a Bird/MessageBird workspace, obtain an AccessKey and SMS channel, and send a first SMS via HTTPS.

first_success.outcome: POST to the Channels messages endpoint accepts an SMS body and returns an accepted message object.
first_success.observable_signal: HTTP 202 JSON message with status such as accepted/processing for the SMS send.

route_selection.surface: Bird Channels API send SMS via HTTPS (AccessKey)
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: Bird Channels API send SMS via HTTPS (AccessKey)

candidates.name: Bird Channels API send SMS via HTTPS (AccessKey)
candidates.reason: MessageBird/Bird messaging docs require an account, active SMS channel, and AccessKey. Sending SMS documents POST https://api.bird.com/workspaces/{workspaceId}/channels/{channelId}/messages with Authorization AccessKey. Prefer HTTPS over SDKs.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to a Bird/MessageBird account and open a workspace.
required_path.observable_result: Channels and API credentials are reachable.

required_path.action: Create or select an active SMS channel and note workspaceId and channelId.
Create an AccessKey and store it securely.
required_path.observable_result: Key value available for Authorization header.

required_path.action: POST https://api.bird.com/workspaces/{workspaceId}/channels/{channelId}/messages with AccessKey and SMS text body/receiver.

uncertainties.question: Exact dashboard labels for creating AccessKey vs legacy MessageBird live/test keys after the Bird rebrand.
uncertainties.impact: Credential UI steps may use Bird naming.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: API docs specify AccessKey header; console labels can change with rebrand.
